name: Trail of the Twelve Apostles
name_original: Ruta de los Doce Apóstoles – circular desde Salvaleón
description: This moderate 10.0-mile mountain biking route in Sierra Suroeste features ancient cork oaks and diverse Mediterranean landscapes near Salval
region_intro: Mountain biking around Salvaleón offers diverse terrain within the Sierra Suroeste region of Extremadura, Spain. The area is characterized by the Sierra de Tentudía, featuring rounded mountains and "dehesas" – Mediterranean woodlands with scattered oak and cork oak trees. Riders can explore varied landscapes, from gentle paths through agroforestry systems to more challenging ascents in the foothills of the Sierra Morena. The region's natural features, including mountains like Risco Barbello and Monte Porrino, provide a rich environment for outdoor exploration. Best mountain bike trails around Salvaleón The most popular mountain bike trail is Salvatierra a Salvaleón a Nogales — circular por el Embalse de Nogales, a 27.5 miles (44.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 3 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views around the Nogales Reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Trail of the Twelve Apostles, a moderate 9.9 miles (16.0 km) path. This trail features rolling terrain through the dehesa landscape. Local mountain bikers also love the Trail of the Twelve Apostles – Nogales Castle loop from Barcarrota, a 31.9 miles (51.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain including the Nogales Castle area, often completed in about 4 hours 25 minutes. Mountain biking around Salvaleón is defined by dehesa woodlands, the Sierra de Tentudía, and numerous hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides through open landscapes to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Salvaleón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 5 reviews. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Salvaleón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when mountain biking around Salvaleón?
A: The terrain around Salvaleón is diverse, ranging from gentle paths through the characteristic "dehesas" (Mediterranean woodlands with scattered oak and cork oak trees) to more challenging ascents in the foothills of the Sierra Morena. You'll encounter rounded mountains, rocky outcrops, and extensive natural heritage areas like Monte Porrino. The varied landscape provides options for different skill levels.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Salvaleón?
A: Yes, Salvaleón offers routes suitable for various abilities. While many trails are moderate, there are 4 identified easy routes that are great for beginners or families looking for a less strenuous ride through the scenic dehesa landscape.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: You can explore a variety of natural features and historical landmarks. Highlights include ancient trees like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1132207" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancient Olive Tree of La Tapada&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3045957" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Twelve Apostles Cork Oaks&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2779557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vulture Cliffs of Risco de Valbellido&lt;/a&gt;, offering opportunities for wildlife spotting, and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6249042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogales Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, Salvaleón offers 11 difficult routes. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24523553" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salvatierra de los Barros – Drinking Water Fountain loop from Salvatierra de los Barros&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 49 km with over 1100 meters of elevation gain, providing a demanding ride through varied terrain.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Salvaleón?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for mountain biking in Salvaleón. The weather is milder and more pleasant than the hot summer, and the landscapes are vibrant. Autumn, in particular, is often promoted for outdoor activities in the Tentudía comarca, suggesting favorable conditions.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes around Salvaleón?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Salvaleón are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1359947973/salvatierra-a-salvaleon-a-nogales-circular-por-el-embalse-de-nogales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salvatierra a Salvaleón a Nogales — circular por el Embalse de Nogales&lt;/a&gt;, a 44.3 km loop offering scenic views around the Nogales Reservoir.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Salvaleón?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 5 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the beautiful dehesa landscapes, and the variety of routes available for different skill levels.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting while mountain biking?
A: Absolutely. The region boasts significant biodiversity, with forests of oak, pine, chestnut, and cork oak providing habitats for various wildlife. You might spot deer, wild boar, genets, foxes, hares, and a variety of birds, including white and black storks, red kites, and booted eagles, especially near areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2779557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vulture Cliffs of Risco de Valbellido&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How long are the mountain bike trails around Salvaleón?
A: The trails vary significantly in length. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030798268/trail-of-the-twelve-apostles" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Twelve Apostles&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 16 km route, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24523613" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle de Matamoros – Valle de Santa Ana loop from Salvaleón&lt;/a&gt; extends to over 51 km, offering options for shorter rides or full-day adventures.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trails in Salvaleón?
A: While specific parking areas for each trail are not detailed, Salvaleón is a small town, and you can typically find parking within the town center or at the start points of popular routes. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for designated parking zones.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the mountain bike trails?
A: Salvaleón and surrounding villages offer local establishments where you can find refreshments and meals. While specific cafes directly on trails are rare, you'll find options in the towns and villages that serve as starting or ending points for many routes, such as Salvaleón itself or nearby Barcarrota.
tips: Rolling through the Sierra Suroeste in the limits of Nogales and Salvaleón, we find one of the many single tracks that we will find in this area, especially if we follow the route of the Twelve Apostles.
Picturesque path that runs along the route of the Twelve Apostles, an ideal route for lovers of hiking and mountain biking, which can be reached from Nogales or, more commonly, from Salvaleón.
Rolling through the area of the Twelve Apostles, we come across this beautiful single track that we mountain bike lovers like so much.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Ruta de los Doce Apóstoles for mountain biking?
faqs.answer: This route is generally considered to be of moderate difficulty for mountain biking. While much of it is accessible, be prepared for at least one steep ascent where you might need to dismount and push your bike. A reasonable level of fitness is beneficial.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily consists of singletracks within the Sierra Suroeste, traversing traditional agroforestry systems known as 'dehesas'. You'll encounter spectacular holm oaks and cork oaks, some centenarian, and ride through Mediterranean scrubland with aromatic plants like thyme and oregano. Features include crossing a stream and passing by a 'Pilar'.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 16 to 17 kilometers long. Given its moderate difficulty and varied terrain, most mountain bikers can expect to complete it in about 2 to 3 hours, depending on their pace and stops.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Ruta de los Doce Apóstoles?
faqs.answer: Since this is a circular route starting and ending in Salvaleón, you can conveniently park within the town. Look for public parking areas in Salvaleón to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta de los Doce Apóstoles mountain bike trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, please ensure your dog is kept on a leash, especially when passing through dehesa areas where livestock may be present, and to protect local wildlife.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to visit are during the spring (March to May) when the landscape is lush and temperatures are mild, or in the autumn (September to November) for pleasant weather and beautiful colors. Summers can be very hot, and winters might be wet.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to ride this trail or enter the Sierra Suroeste?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to mountain bike the Ruta de los Doce Apóstoles or to access the Sierra Suroeste area where it is located. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The highlight is undoubtedly the 'Doce Apóstoles' themselves – a group of ancient cork oaks, of which seven still stand. You'll also ride through beautiful dehesa landscapes with holm and cork oaks, and experience the Singletrack in the Sierra Suroeste and the specific Trail of the Twelve Apostles. Keep an eye out for occasional historical elements like Roman ruins or megalithic monuments.

faqs.question: Does this mountain bike route connect with any other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route does intersect with other paths. Approximately 10% of the route follows sections of the official 'Camino de Barcarrota a Nogales' trail.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the trailhead in Salvaleón?
faqs.answer: Yes, Salvaleón is a small town and offers local establishments where you can find refreshments or a meal before or after your ride. It's a good idea to check local opening hours, especially outside of peak tourist seasons.

faqs.question: What is the 'Doce Apóstoles' and why is it significant?
faqs.answer: The 'Doce Apóstoles' (Twelve Apostles) refers to a group of twelve centenarian cork oaks that are a central feature and namesake of the trail. These ancient trees are iconic landmarks within the dehesa landscape, though only seven of the original twelve still stand today.
